entry.title: Re: Printing a single page from multiple pages using Crys...
entry.content:
I haven't seen the new print dialog Windows 11 is using. Bring it up and then hold the ALT key and hit PRTSC key to copy it and paste into the post.

Crystal Reports Designer uses this one:

CR for VS uses these Windows API's to get the Printer info:

System.Drawing.Printing.PrintDocument pDoc = new System.Drawing.Printing.PrintDocument();
System.Drawing.Printing.PageSettings page = new System.Drawing.Printing.PageSettings();

entry.title: Re: BSP change value in the grid by iterator

entry.title: Re: Error Message - Server is busy
entry.content: Unfortunately, the user can no longer say which printer he used. The print drivers are up to date. A report with several pages has been printed. If by Windows display you mean the Windows event log, then we can assure you that this has always been inconspicuous in previous server busy messages.

entry.title: Re: Connecting to csv files in Crystal 2020 using the 64...
entry.content:
In 64 BIT ODBC Admin tool click on the Driver tab and see if you have these:

Either the CR Textfile ODBC or Microsoft Access Driver.

If you don't you may need 64 Bit Office installed or a third party 64 bit CSV client.

To create the DSN:

Configure ODBC:
  1. Open the 64-bit ODBC application in your system.
  2. Click on Add button present in "User DSN".
  3. Select "Microsoft Access Text Driver (*. ...
  4. Enter a logical name for the ODBC data source, ...
  5. Navigate to the folder/directory where your CSV file is present. ...
  6. Click "OK" and close the ODBC Text Setup.

entry.title: Re: Top menu toolbar is not clickable
entry.content:
I have exactly the same issue. It happens only for some themes like Quartz and Belize. For "older" themes it's fine. 

Started after upgrade to Windows 11. Before it worked fine.

Any idea?

entry.title: CAP Multitenancy without DB?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I was wondering if it’s possible to build a multi-tenant application in CAPM without the database module. In my use case, I don’t need any persistent database to store or retrieve data, as the data comes from an OData service configured in the destination. From the multi-tenant perspective, I want to handle subscription, un subscription, route creation, etc.

Is this possible in BTP CAP?

entry.title: Re: SAP SD Sales Certification
entry.content:
Hi @Swastika11 
SAP Certification exam, one attempts is not available in every region for purchase.
To get certified, you can purchase the new SAP Certification exam, two attempts offering, that offers two exam attempts and 10 hours of access to practice systems.
This offering, however, does not include access to stay certified assessments. To stay certified, you need an SAP Learning Hub subscription, otherwise you need to complete the full certification exam every year, once your SAP Certification expires.
For more information, please visit the FAQ.

Best regards,
Margit

entry.title: Re: How to develop run time designer crystal report in wi...
entry.content:
Go here and get the latest SP to integrate into VS 2010->2022:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

Download the sample app and reference materials:

Related Documents

Crystal Reports for Visual Studio Licensing

Getting started and moving ahead with Crystal Reports .NET applications

See this Blog for instructions upgrading your existing project to the latest SP

See this blog on how to use CR 2020 Export to XLXS page formats:

How to use the new CR 2020 export to Microsoft Excel(XLXS) in Visual Studio .NET code (new)

how-to-parameters-in-crystal-reports-for-visual-studio-net

Printing Crystal Reports in .NET

SAP Crystal Reports for Visual Studio Installation Guide ( SP 21 and above)

You will be using RAS, Report Application Server, to make changes to your report.

How you make those changes is up to you, I don't recall anyone providing an example.

entry.title: Frage zu Note 3458956: Integration Leistungsgruppen in BW-Healthcare-Content
entry.content:
Die Note 3458956 beschreibt die Integration der Leistungsgruppen für die Erstellung des §21-Datensatzes:

Mit den Support Packages im März 2025 wurden Funktionen/Anpassungen zur Verfügung gestellt, damit

  • die Stammdaten der Leistungsgruppen im System hinterlegt werden können,
  • das Programm RNAP21K01 dazu in der Lage ist, die beiden neuen Dateien KRANKENHAUSSTRUKTUR_LEISTUNGSGRUPPEN und ARZTQUALIFIKATION_LEISTUNGSGRUPPEN erstellen zu können und
  • das Programm RNAP21F01 dazu in der Lage ist, in der Datei FALL die Spalten LG-Bezug und Leistungsgruppe erstellen zu können.

Für uns ergeben sich die Frage: Wird es eine Integration der Leistungsgruppen in den BW-Healthcare-Content geben? Wann wird damit zu rechnen sein?

entry.title: Am not able to see coupons in promotions conditions & action section.
entry.content:
I have enabled the OOTB promotion and coupons extensions in my project setup. 

I am able to create customer coupons and single-code / multicode coupons in my backoffice. 

But am unable to create a promotion with coupon conditions. Am only seeing cart , customer and general options in condition section. Am unable to see the coupon option there. Below are the extension I have used to enable the promotions and coupons

<!-- Start ::: coupons-->
<extension name="couponbackoffice"/>
<extension name="couponfacades"/>
<extension name="couponwebservices"/>
<extension name="couponservices"/>
<!-- End ::: coupons-->

<!-- base-commerce -->
<extension name="basecommerce"/>
<extension name="basecommercebackoffice"/>
<extension name="customerreview"/>
<extension name="payment"/>
<extension name="voucherbackoffice"/>
<extension name="voucher"/>
<extension name="promotionsbackoffice"/>
<extension name="promotions"/>
<!-- promotion-engine -->
<extension name="promotionenginebackoffice"/>
<extension name="promotionengineservices"/>
<!-- rule-engine -->
<extension name="ruledefinitions"/>
<extension name="droolsruleengineservices"/>
<extension name="ruleenginebackoffice"/>
<extension name="rulebuilderbackoffice"/>
<extension name="ruleengine"/>

entry.title: Re: Preview report translation error
entry.content:
Hi there,

a question: Do you have "Value" translations for the data that you expect to be translated? Note that we currently only support a certain set of value translations - you can see the full list here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_admin/value-translations.html?locale=en-US&q=value+translation 

Best regards,

Franzisca

entry.title: Re: S/4 Public cloud - Service - recurring service for in...
entry.content:
Hello,

To trigger an in-house repair process, you need to generate an appropriate object in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition. Here’s how you can manage this:

Options for In-House Repair Process Initiation

1. Customer Returns

The in-house repair process can be initiated through customer returns. You can generate a customer returns order that can serve as the basis for receiving equipment for repair. This approach directly ties the in-house repair process Service - In-House Repair with logistics and customer return operations.

2. Manage In-House Repairs App

Utilize the Manage In-House Repairs app to create in-house repairs. This app allows you to directly add repair objects, such as equipment marked for in-house repair and maintenance, without generating a standard service order.

Step-by-Step Process:

  • Create Customer Returns: Initiate the repair process by creating a customer return, which includes the items to be repaired.
  • Add Repair Objects: Using the Manage In-House Repairs app, you can add repair objects marked with a serial number, thus positioning them for an in-house repair process.

Challenges with Service Orders

Attempting to trigger the repair process through service orders, SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 - In-House Repair specifically SVO1 service orders, may not be feasible as these orders are generally aligned with different operations rather than in-house repair-specific actions.

Workaround

If direct generation of service orders leads to SVO1 service orders, the workaround involves:

  • Creating Returns Orders: Initiate with a returns order that can be processed as an in-house repair order.
  • Utilizing Templates: Configure the repair template within the Manage In-House Repairs app to bypass service order generation and correctly associate the repair objects with the in-house repair process. Service - Manage the Returns Process for Repair Objects

Integration

For seamless integration, ensure your system is configured to recognize and process in-house repair requests efficiently. Using the Manage In-House Repairs app and customer returns approach ensures all logistics and service operations are aligned with the specific needs of your customer’s equipment repair operations.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Calculating Energy Consumption in the Product Carbon Footprint
entry.content:
Hi,

You can either allocate the actual energy consumption or a planned consumption in SFM. Check this help section for the different options:
https://help.sap.com/docs/sustainability-footprint-management/scenario-guide-for-sap-sustainability-footprint-management-93545a51dfb84c228a636b9d01c23707/calculate-baseline-emissions?locale=en-US

Best regards,
Dietmar

entry.title: Re: Architecture overview of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)
entry.content: Nice Overview of BCD. Thanks. @AliMehedi

entry.title: Bank accounts cannot be deleted
entry.content:
Hello Community,
I am trying to delete the old bank details of a business partner, but I always get the message: Data cannot be deleted because deletion is deactivated.
However, it works on the test system (settings are actually identical).
Could you tell me where I can set this?
Many thanks for your support,

Güli

entry.title: Re: S/4 Public cloud - Service - Ship spare part for a se...
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the process of shipping spare parts to a customer as part of a service order involves integration between service order management and logistics. Here Serial number management using Cloud Warehouse Man… - SAP Community is a comprehensive overview:

Service Order Management Process

  1. Adding Spare Parts to Service Order:

    • When you add a service part defined as a stock material to a service order, the system checks the availability in the default storage location assigned to the plant.

  2. Automatic Reservation:

    • You can configure when the system makes reservations for stock service part items based on the lifecycle status Service - Logistics Integration in the service transaction. This helps ensure that the necessary spare parts are reserved for the order.

  3. Assigning Employees:

    • If the service order is assigned to a technician with a specific storage location, the logistics process must ensure stock is transferred to this location using the ‘Transfer Stock’ app.

Logistics Integration Process

  1. Shipping Spare Parts to Customer:

    • Spare parts can be shipped following a similar process as in the sales module by leveraging stock transport orders (STOs).
    • The STO approach involves creating an order that facilitates inventory checks, spare part reservations, and transfer from the main storage to another storage location if needed.

  2. Using Configuration Activities:

    • Define lifecycle statuses and storage location assignments using SAP’s configuration activities to manage item categories and logistics transactions effectively.

  3. Item Categories:

    • Various item categories can be used, including ‘SVR1’ for stock service parts which ensure parts are managed as inventory items throughout the service process.

Workaround for Spare Parts That Cannot Be Carried

  • Transfer Stocks: Logistics could use ‘Transfer Stock’ processes to move spare parts directly from storage to the customer’s location.
  • Shipment Address: The service parts can be shipped to a different address if specified under assignment blocks like ‘Service Part Recipient’, allowing flexibility in direct shipment.

These processes together ensure the effective integration of service orders with logistics, enabling seamless operations and customer satisfaction even for parts that are Service - Procurement of Non-Stock Service Parts bulky or heavy.

Best regards

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello @MLogunov,

Thank you for your insightful comment. This approach certainly adds another dimension to handling business scenarios where event-based filtering is required. 

Thanks again for your valuable contribution.

entry.title: Re: S/4 Public cloud - Service - repair in house subcontr...
entry.content:
Hello,

Managing in-house repair processes that involve subcontractors in SAP S/4HANA Cloud can be efficiently handled using the In-House Repair business process Enhance your Customer Experience and Streamline Pr… - SAP Community along with some additional steps for subcontractor engagements.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud In-House Repair

Service Perspective

  • Precheck: When the customer’s equipment is received, an in-house repair order is created. This step includes checking the equipment and documenting the necessary repair actions.
  • Repair Planning: Technicians plan the repair tasks and can decide if subcontractor assistance is needed for specific tasks.

Purchasing Perspective

  • Subcontractor Engagement: You can use purchase orders to manage subcontracting. SAP allows the creation of a purchase order for the subcontractor, specifying the tasks they need to perform.
  • Procurement of Services: Integrate the purchase order with SAP Field Service Management Crowd Services - Expand Your Talent Pool with SAP … - SAP Community for detailing the services required from the subcontractor.

Logistics Perspective

  • Shipment to Subcontractor: Use outbound delivery functionality to send the equipment to the subcontractor. Ensure that this is documented for tracking.
  • Receiving Equipment: After the subcontractor completes their tasks, receive the equipment using inbound logistics functionalities, ensuring a smooth handover for the final checks.

Workaround

If standard functionalities don’t cover all aspects, potential workarounds include:

  • Detailed Documentation and Manual Oversight: Ensure all steps are documented for tracking and audits.
  • Use Workflow Alerts: Set up workflow alerts for notifying relevant stakeholders about subcontractor engagements and equipment statuses.

By leveraging SAP’s existing logistics and service capabilities, the process can be streamlined effectively, enhancing transparency and efficiency in managing in-house In-House Repair in S/4HANA Service - Start with an… - SAP Community repairs involving subcontractors.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Scrolling table control
entry.content:
Debug your code and you'll see what happens.

Probably that pressing the Enter key has the same effect (leaving the screen). Check that the dynpro attribute "next screen" is not blank/zero (i.e. leaves the screen). The attribute "next screen" of one screen NNNN is usually to be the same screen number NNNN. A change of the next screen may also happen at runtime via SET SCREEN or LEAVE TO SCREEN.

For your information, scrolling in a table control does automatically 2 things:

  1. Change the TOP_LINE number in your CONTROLS-based variable, to the new top line
  2. Simulate pressing the Enter key

entry.title: Re: How to Extract PDF files data in SAP S4 HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi munipkumar,

Did you already look into Document Information Extraction? It's a SAP BTP service that you can call everywhere in developer extensibility, where you can call an HTTP API. This would be my start and I would just build something completely custom, if there are complex requirements that can't be fulfilled by the service.

https://help.sap.com/docs/document-information-extraction

BR,
Alex

entry.title: Re: Long bank account number for china vendor (more than...
entry.content: Yes, Under cdtr>acct we have defined two nodes of ID to FPAYH-ZBNKN and the length of fields are maintained as zero

entry.title: Re: Error Message - Server is busy
entry.content: Are they real printers or Microsofts Easy Printer (Redirected). CR requires the real printer driver to be installed due to required access to the Registry and DEVMODE structures. CR is simply passing the error message from the Printer to you in a message box, CR has no control over the buffer size the printer uses. The message is coming from the Printer and simply telling you the Printer is too busy to take another print job.

entry.title: Re: Everything you ever wanted to know about the Migration from the Peppol Exchange service to the n

We have migrated to the new version of PEPPOL without any issues, creating new subaccounts without having to activate Cloud Foundry.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: S/4 Public cloud - Service - solution order and repai...
entry.content:
Hello,

The in-house repair does not directly fit into the solution order, if you want to track all the 3 steps in one. 

You may still create 3 service orders in one solution order. You then need to link the service order for step 2 to an in-house repair. You use the step 2 service order to track the costs and time and use the in-house repair to execute the repair process. 

Or you integrate the in-house repair directly to the solution order, linking the cost and equipment. But this is not supported by standard.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Why is FIORI Elements application is not restoring the state on page refresh
entry.content:
Please find the details below.

1) Existing version details where it works fine.

SAPUI5: 1.60.14,  FIORI FE Server: 2018,   Application Type: FIORI Elements application (List Report floor plan)

2) Upgraded version where the application is failing

SAPUI5: 1.120.0,   FIORI FE Server: 2023

Problem Statement: In the upgraded system, the list report loads fine and am able to navigate to the item detail (Object page). But when I refresh, the object page does not load (blank screen). One thing I have observed with earlier version versus upgraded version is that, in the earlier version the launchpad framework adds sap-iapp-state to the url but in the upgraded version the launchpad also adds sap-iapp-state--history besides sap-iapp-state.

What I sense is either I am not able to rightly configure 'sap-iapp-state-history' (if there is any explicit config needed) or there is some issue with it. Problem is, I am not able to find a way to disable 'sap-iapp-state--history' and then test how the application is behaving.

entry.content:

Thanks

Naveen

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content: Hi @antonclv it usually takes a few hours until it is updated. Please wait until the next day - latest then it should be updated. Kind Regards, Suzanne

entry.title: Re: SAP Mobile Start fails to load App
entry.content:
Hi Diego,

thanks a lot for your comment and insights. Just want to update on this. We are aware of this issue and working on a fix. The current official documentation is not a new approach but an option how to make currently use of SAP Mobile Start. Our goal is to have this again automatically created / updated. We will keep you posted about it and latest once the fix is deployed.

Please reach out in case of any additional comments / issues.

Thanks and best regards

Patrick

entry.title: No Identity Provisioning on IAS
entry.content:
I am using an existing Internal IAS that I did not set up by request and it has no Identity Provisioning.
The"Manage Identity Provisioning" role is missing is not available or listed for Admins in the IAS Admin console.

Does it need to be bundled with IAS when initially requested?
From my understanding it is now auto included with IAS.

This is no help 3253894 - Identity Authentication Service "Manage Identity Provisioning" role is missing 
I am on a CF BTP Sub account and want to better understand with the demise of SAML2 on sub accounts for Trust to IAS.

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hi @AntonioNieto 

what is meant is that this certification C_TFIN52 is not in the portfolio anymore and for this expires. The alternative certification is a recommendation to take in order to keep the certified status but it is another certification than C_TFIN52 so you need to get certified.

You can schedule the slot for the recommended alternative via  SAP Certified Associate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Fina  
via pressing the "Schedule exam" button:

Kind Regards, Suzanne

entry.title: Re: Cloud API Enablement - FAQ
entry.content:
Hi @AlexanderRother 

I would like to know if there has been any change in the use of RFC in the clean core model in relation to S/4HANA Cloud Private?.....For example, do we still consider it as Tier 3? Or do we have another Tier classification?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: PI REST Adapter - Custom error handling
entry.content:
Hi, 

I am having an issue with this option in PO.

The rest service to who I am calling to is returning an empty message when the data is incorrect including info in custom headers. 

This result in an exception in po: java.io.IOException: java.io.EOFException

Using the Text Content -> REsult message is empty, I keep getting the EOFException.

The only way I can avoid this, is using the Exception option in error handling. It should be ok for us, but when using the Exception option in the Error handling tab, we get the following exception in message monitoring:

java.lang.NullPointerException: while trying to invoke the method java.lang.CharSequence.toString() of a null object loaded from local variable 'replacement'

Any Idea on how to resolve the last one?

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hi Admin,

 

Our People have completed the Delta however their Professional level certification is being shown as getting expired by 31 march 2025. How can we renew our professional certification.

entry.title: Europe´s 1st Two-Tier SAP Cloud ERP Summit
entry.content:
WALLDORF - On February 18th and 19th, we held the 1st European Two-Tier Cloud ERP Summit, a 1,5-day workshop for partners focusing on connected Large Enterprise scenarios updating also on the future GTM with SAP Business Suite. 

 The purpose of the event was to educate and raise the attention to watch out for such scenarios in architecture discussions with larger clients. It also provided great showcases and increased the credibility and trust in Two-tier ERP operating models. 

 With over 60 participants from EMEA & MEE countries mainly from GSSP (Deloitte, IBM, Accenture etc.) as well as some architects from European VAR´s we had a great turn-up ratio of 90%. 

 The workshop provided our partners with insights and knowledge around the SAP Two-Tier ERP Cloud strategy and popular scenarios, whilst we also deep dived into the architectural guidelines and integrations. Especially, the customer proof points from Bosch, LanXess and Accelleron presented by our partners Deloitte and Accenture resonated very well with our partners. 

 The workshop also helped us foster open and trusted conversations during in-person exchanges between SAP subject matter experts and partner leadership teams.  

Due to high demand among our GSSP´s, we will keep the momentum and plan to provide this workshop in more regions. Let us know if you want to contribute to the next Summits and please contact Nitin Vohra or Nilguen Atasoy.

 “The SAP Two tier Cloud summit allowed a common facilitated workspace where vendors could come together, discuss and learn about how to make SAP S/4 HANA public cloud a success among their clients by learning from each other and SAP. It was valuable in both generating confidence & providing new insights.” - Jonathan Eaton, Senior Specialist Lead | Deloitte Certified Chief Architect |

Deloitte

entry.title: Join our next Espresso Session on SAP Wipro sto360
entry.content:
Join us for the upcoming espresso session to discover how SAP sto360 can revolutionize your asset-intensive Shutdowns, Turnarounds, and Outages (STOs).

Here’s what you can look forward to:

❓ Why Attend?

  • Optimize Performance and Costs: Learn about predictive maintenance and improved cost control strategies that can boost performance and reduce operational costs.
  • Enhanced Security and Integration: Understand why SAP Endorsed Apps like sto360 are at the forefront of security, performance, and seamless integration.
  • Proactive STO Management: Explore the transformative potential of SAP Wipro sto360 in shifting to a proactive management approach, optimizing efficiency, mitigating risks, and enhancing overall performance.

💡 What You Will Learn:

  • How predictive maintenance and cost control can transform your asset-centered operations.
  • The role of SAP Endorsed Apps in ensuring high security and integration standards.
  • The ways SAP sto360 can elevate your STO management to be more proactive, efficient, and risk-averse.

🎤 Meet Our Speakers:

  • Tiago Fernandes - Business Development Manager, Assets & Service Management, SAP Tiago brings extensive expertise in managing assets and service within the SAP ecosystem.

  • Hisham Gouda - Chief Solution Expert for Energy, Utilities, Engineering, and Construction industries, Shutdown Turnaround Outage, SAP with a wealth of experience in the Energy and Utilities sector, 

🔔 Save the date and register

Date: Wednesday, 26 March 2025
Time: 16:00 Central European Time
Duration: 30 minutes

🤝 We look forward to seeing you at our espresso session!

entry.title: Re: Payroll PCR to get Weekly/Annual Working hours
entry.content: I Believe we need to write the rule based on VARB by using TABLE508A operation. Why cannot we change rule to get Hourly Rate for monthly? RTE=TKSOLL and RTE/TKDIVI we can also use the constant from Table T511K

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements: Filter based on association property
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I stumbled upon this question because I had a very similar requirement: adding a filter on the list report page for a property of a child entity (i.e. filtering on an associated entity with a one-to-many cardinality). In my RAP BO and Fiori Elements app, I eventually managed to solve this using a virtual element with a custom filter.

In the implementation of the filter transformation method (if_sadl_exit_filter_transform~map_atom), I selected all entries from the child entity where the relevant field contains the filter value (iv_value). Next, I looped over the retrieved entries and, for each one, created a condition on the field used to match the association between the parent and the child. (In my scenario this is also the unique key of the parent entity, so I could directly filter on this field, but perhaps there are some use cases where it is necessary to first obtain the corresponding entries of the parent entity). I then chained these individual conditions together using the OR operator, as explained on this SAP Help page. Finally, I added a fallback condition to handle the case when no matching entries are found, ensuring that the filter is never empty.

Below is a generic code sample where you can replace the placeholders (e.g. <your_child_entity>, <matching_field>, <parent_key>, and <filter_field>) with your actual names.

I hope this solution will help anyone else with the same requirement!

 

 

METHOD if_sadl_exit_filter_transform~map_atom.

  DATA lt_child_entries TYPE TABLE OF <your_child_entity>.
  DATA ls_child_entry   TYPE <your_child_entity>.
  DATA lo_field_filter  TYPE REF TO if_sadl_simple_cond_element.
  DATA lo_condition     TYPE REF TO if_sadl_cond_provider_generic.

  " Get all entries from the child entity where <filter_field> equals iv_value
  SELECT <matching_field> FROM <your_child_entity>
    WHERE <filter_field> = _value
    INTO TABLE _child_entries.

  LOOP AT lt_child_entries INTO ls_child_entry.
    " Create a condition for the parent entity key field
    lo_field_filter = cl_sadl_cond_prov_factory_pub=>create_simple_cond_factory( )->element( '<parent_key>' ).
    lo_condition = lo_field_filter->equals( ls_child_entry-<matching_field> ).

    " Chain conditions with OR
    IF ro_condition IS INITIAL.
      ro_condition = lo_condition.
    ELSE.
      ro_condition = ro_condition->or( lo_condition ).
    ENDIF.

    CLEAR lo_field_filter.
  ENDLOOP.

  " Fallback: If no conditions were created, apply a default condition
  IF ro_condition IS INITIAL.
    lo_field_filter = cl_sadl_cond_prov_factory_pub=>create_simple_cond_factory( )->element( '<parent_key>' ).
    ro_condition = lo_field_filter->equals( '' ).
  ENDIF.

ENDMETHOD.

Margit

entry.title: Re: Payroll PCR to get Weekly/Annual Working hours
entry.content: why cannot we change rule to get Hourly Rate for monthly ? RTE=TKSOLL and RTE/TKDIVI

entry.title: Celebrating Innovation and Empowerment: SAP Women in Tech and DSAG Host Fourth Innovation Day
entry.content:
On March 12th, the SAP Women in Tech team, in collaboration with DSAG (German-speaking SAP User Group), hosted the fourth Innovation Day event. This annual gathering brought together businesswomen from SAP, SAP partners and customers to share insights, network, and explore the latest trends in technology. With a focus on artificial intelligence (AI), this year’s event highlighted the transformative potential of AI across industries and its impact on women in the professional world.

Key Highlights

The event featured a range of engaging sessions, including

  • The Secret to Happy Customers – Why Product-Centricity is the Winning Recipe,
  • LLMs gone rouge: When prompts turn from helpful to harmful,
  • Connecting Innovation and Society for a Sustainable Agricultural Future, and
  • Rethinking Leadership: Where Empathy Meets Technology

All the sessions were powerful reminders of how innovation can drive positive changes across industries.

Another highlight was a panel discussion on the opportunities AI brings for women in the professional world. Panelists explored how AI is creating new career paths, enabling women to take on leadership roles, and fostering diversity in tech. The discussion emphasized the importance of ensuring AI development reflects diverse perspectives to build inclusive solutions.

My Take-aways:

  1. AI is a Game-Changer Across Industries: The event highlighted real-world examples of how AI is transforming how we work and live. While we enjoy the convenience and significantly improved efficiency with the help of different AI tools and agents in certain areas of our daily work, never forget to balance our health.
  2. AI needs a human touch: While AI is powerful, its true potential is realized when combined with human creativity, empathy, and ethical oversight. 
  3. Networking and Collaboration are Key: Events like Innovation Day provide invaluable opportunities for women to connect, share ideas, and support one another in their professional journeys.

Many of the participants stayed long after the event was over. I for one can already not wait for next year when we meet again. Here’s to many more years of innovation, collaboration, and empowerment!

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA DB Performance Tunning.
entry.content:
Dear sir,

Thanks for the detailed informative blog. 

I want to add one delta merge point which impact the performace of HANA .

To keep control of the delta merge we keep our table enable AUTOMERGE but in the data loading time we keep it into disable auto merge and enable smart merge for large volume tables.

Auto Merge :  HANA keeps a compressed “main” store of column tables and an uncompressed “delta” store, for new items. Periodically, a process called mergedog combines the two in a DELTA MERGE process. Sometimes however your tables may not be configured to automerge (you can issue ALTER TABLE table name ENABLE AUTOMERGE), and occasionally merged fails and you have to restart HANA. This can cause the delta store to become very large, and because it is uncompressed, this is a big issue if you don’t have much RAM.

**---------------------------------------------------------------

ALTER TABLE “SCHEMA”.”TABLE” DISABLE AUTOMERGE;

IMPORT FROM ‘/sapmnt/log/test.ctl’ WITH THREADS 120 BATCH 200000;

MERGE DELTA OF “SCHEMA”.”TABLE”;

ALTER TABLE “SCHEMA”.”TABLE ENABLE AUTOMERGE;

**---------------------------------------------------------------

 Thanks & Regards,

Abhishek Chaudhary

entry.title: Re: Long bank account number for china vendor (more than...

entry.title: Re: How to Setup Direct connection from On Prem to SAP Logistics Business Network Freight Collaborat
entry.content: Nice tutorial.

entry.title: Null pointer Exception when using Error handling in rest receiver channel im SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi, 

My scenario is proxy -> synchronous REST.

The REST service I'm calling returns an empty message when the input data is incorrect, including error information in custom headers.

This generates an exception in PO: java.io.IOException: java.io.EOFException

In the error handling tab in the rest receiver channel, when using "Text Content" -> "Result Message is Empty", I still get an EOFException.

The only way to work around this is to use the "Exception" option in "error handling". It should work fine, but when using the "Exception" option in the "Error Handling" tab, we get the following exception in message monitoring:

java.lang.NullPointerException: When trying to invoke the java.lang.CharSequence.toString() method on a null object loaded from the local variable 'replacement'

Any ideas on how to resolve this null pointer exception?

It happens no matter what kind of message I put in "Message Content".

Thanks And regards.

Alex-

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS - concat_with_space - concatenate with dynamic spaces
entry.content:
Genius... this did the trick for me.

Although it's not elegent and easy to understand with human eyes, but certainly works quite well.

this is how I used it for my requirement:

replace( replace( concat( concat(concat( rpad( ltrim(afpo.matnr, '0'), 18 , '#' ), afpo.pwerk ), afpo.verid), '##########S' ), '#', ' #' ), '#', '') as SOURCE_ID,

the outcome, with exact spaces in th middle as required:

1000008-000001    P0010001          S

Thanks,

Bharat

Do you know how to get this to pull through and map from RCM to ONB? Any assistance would be greatly appreciated. Many thanks, Caitlin

entry.title: Re: Italy eDocument for zero value invoices
entry.content:
Hi Vishnu,

I couldn't find a KBA to support my answer, but In Italy, the electronic invoicing system, known as FatturaPA, often requires invoices to have a non-zero monetary value. This is likely the reason why the eDocument transmission is failing for zero-value invoices but succeeding for those with a nominal value, such as 0.01 EUR.
Zero-value invoices may not be accepted by the system due to compliance regulations, as the system is designed to capture transactions with financial value. Therefore, this requirement to use at least a nominal value when selling samples might be a workaround for any limitations in the invoicing framework used in Italy.

Still, have you tried using the full amount of the product and provide a 100% discount? That might work as well.

All the best,

Eduardo

entry.title: Which YCOA Account to Select for Planned Cost in Project with Revenue (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Publ
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and creating a project with revenue. While uploading the planned cost via Excel, I'm unsure which YCOA (Chart of Accounts) account I should select for accurate cost allocation.

Here are some specifics about my situation:

  • I’m importing the planned cost through the "Import Financial Plan Data" app.
  • The main confusion is about selecting the correct G/L account that aligns with the YCOA chart.

Could anyone advise on:

  1. Which criteria should I consider when choosing the correct YCOA account for planned costs?
  2. Is there a recommended list of accounts typically used for revenue projects in this context?
  3. How can I ensure the selected account aligns correctly with financial reporting and project cost tracking?

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your support. 😊

entry.title: Which Currency Should Be Used for Uploading Cost and Budget in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and have a question regarding cost and budget uploads for projects with Revenue.

When uploading planned costs and budgets via the "Import Financial Plan Data" app, I’m unsure whether I should:

  • Use the project currency defined during project creation, or
  • Upload the data in the global currency (e.g., CHF), which is set as the group currency.

Could anyone clarify:

  1. Is it mandatory to upload costs and budgets in the global currency, or can the project currency be used?
  2. If only the global currency is accepted, how is currency conversion handled for reporting in the project currency?
  3. Are there any best practices for ensuring consistency in financial reporting and project tracking across multiple currencies?

Any insights or suggestions from your experiences would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you for your support. 😊

entry.title: inspection lots not created in "Released" status in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
I have maintained an inspection plan for a raw material by uploading it using the migration cockpit. Then, an inspection lot was generated automatically, but it is showing as in 'Created' status ( CRTD CHCR SPRQ ). I maintained the inspection plan, then tried to assign it to the inspection lot, but it is showing 'Inspection plan not assigned.' Initially, the inspection lot was showing the current date, but after I used the 'Task/Specification Assign' option, it changed to a past date. How can I resolve this issue?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: inspection lots not created in "Released" status in S...
entry.content:
Please check the KBA Popup with "No inspection plan could be found" (message QA278) when using 'Manage Inspection Lot' app to explain 

 

If a material has a revision level assigned , the key date of the revision level is used to find a valid inspection plan to use.

 

If the key date is before the valid from date of the inspection plan created then the inspection plan will not be assigned.

 

The KBA outlines how to update the Key date of the Revision Level or how to assign the inspection plan manually

entry.title: Upcoming Webcast: Enhancing Your Waste Management with Advanced Planning and Dispatching
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community,

Are you interested to learn how to enhance your waste mangement? 

 

 

 

 

 

You are invited to join us for an insightful Information session:  

Title: Enhance Your Waste Management with Advanced Planning and Dispatching
📅 Date: Tuesday, 18 March 2025
⏰ Time: 16:00 Central European Time
🕒 Duration: 30 minutes 

🔍 What to expect: SAP and PROLOGA are revolutionizing waste management with our new automated route planning and dispatching solution! Streamline your daily service orders by optimizing routes, planning, and dispatching personnel, and dynamically adjusting plans in real-time.

🌟 Why attend?

  • Discover the capabilities of the enhanced SAP Add-On
  • Witness use cases and key benefits, including:
    • 💰Cost reduction by optimizing fleet capacities
    • 🌳CO2 reduction through optimized routes
  • Engage with industry experts

🎤 Speakers:

  • Miquel Carbo – Director Water and Waste Management, SAP
  • Christoph Hillmann – VP Circular Economy & CoDevelopment (Member of the Executive Board), PROLOGA GmbH

💻Don't miss out! Register now and learn how to enhance your waste management operations. 🌟

entry.title: Document Reporting & compliance general questions
entry.content:
Hi DRC Experts, 

I am an FI Consultant who wants to check if DRC is applicable for one of our clients (SAP S4/Hana on Premise). 

I see that SAP uses Peppol Solution heavily in their DRC supported Scenarios. 

Assuming the suppliers dont have Peppol access point, how can DRC Process following scenarios:

  1. Incoming supplier invoices (Paper & PDF) 
  2. Incoming Purchases orders (PDF or XML) 

then I have some specific questions which I couldnt find explicitly in the SAP Documentation:

  1. how does DRC chooses the communication method (email or Peppol) for sending E-invoices?
  2. how does DRC chooses the E-invoice format (ZUGFERD , X-Rechnung etc.)?
  3. Is it possible to send same E-Invoice to multiple customers? how? 

These are critical questions to anwer before we can suggest DRC Solution to our client. 

Thanks for your expertise. 

Regards

Rashed

Hello MDG Experts.

There are two processes to do some mass changes in MDG.

1 - File Upload app

2 - Mass Change app

I need to change the field "LINE ITEM DISPLAY" in GL ACCOUNT Table ( this is a staging table ) 

The technical field is  USMDZ1_XKRES and the type is XFELD.

The problem is, The apps is not making any changes. I received the message saying "ok". I approve all Change Request created for it, but is not reflecting the change.

For other fields with the same type working correctly. The problem is only for "Line Item Display".

Do you guys know if there are any configuration in SPRO for example to configure it or how to fix it ?

Thank you!

entry.title: Binding repeated user inputs into the data variable collection
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I have designed a table with 4 columns, of which two columns (lets say field A & B) would be displaying data coming from the data variable collection (lets say - dataVar), while other two columns (lets say - field C & D) are to be made available for the users to add the comments. I wish to add the repeated user maintained data in column C & D, to be populated in the data variable collection - dataVar, so that I can have all the collection of all 4 fields formatted under json structured to be triggered to the backend system.

I tried using few known functions but no data is getting populated for C & D due to some missing piece. Can someone shed some light on how these functionality could be achieved? 

Thanks in advance.

BR, Chandan

entry.title: Re: Syntax error in program SAPLWR11
entry.content:
For such error, perform a search for a note that update ddic with the field or structure/table name (e.g. DDIC objects FLIFTXT )

  • You should find notes such as 3576066 - DDIC objects for the note 3575476 (or one of the currently imported notes)

entry.title: Hello SAP Community!

We’re conducting market research for Datalark — an SAP-centric platform designed to simplify data migration, integration, and transformation without heavy IT involvement. If you work with SAP data, we’d love your insights!

💡 Who we’re looking for:
✅ SAP professionals using Syniti, Precisely, or similar tools with SAP systems
✅ SAP consultants engaged in data quality, migration, integration, and data management activities

🗓 Why participate?

- Influence the development of an innovative SAP data platform
- Get an exclusive look at Datalark’s capabilities
- Our gratitude (and a small thank-you gift 🎁)

Interested? Comment below or email me at: leanid.hasan@leverx.com

entry.title: Get Involved: Help Shape the Future of SAP Globalization in 2025
entry.content:
As businesses expand globally, navigating local regulations, legal mandates, and statutory reporting becomes complex. SAP Globalization is here to help by providing a comprehensive localization solution across SAP’s portfolio, ensuring companies can succeed both locally and globally.

The first cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, offers you as SAP customers and partners the chance to directly influence future localization features. By joining, you’ll exchange feedback with SAP product management to shape the future of SAP Globalization.

Registration for SAP Globalization projects is open until March 28, 2025. Don’t miss out on this opportunity to help define the future of global business solutions. Follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities or check out the full list here:

  • Australia and New Zealand: GST requirement for Down payments
  • Australia: Modified Payment Times Report - AASB 8 and Taxable Payments Annual Report
  • ISO 21378 Audit Data Collection Standard
  • Planned enhancements to Run statutory reports to handle Draft Report reconciliation
  • Sales and Use Tax Reporting in United States - User Research
  • Sales and Use Tax payments in United States

We believe that collaboration is key to sparking innovation. By working alongside our customers and partners, we can jointly shape the future of SAP’s products and transform business operations.

Let’s take this journey together. Together, we have the power to drive progress and transform industries.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Re: Refreshing the Consumption Item Mapping when a Field...
entry.content: Hi Eric,

entry.title: Re: Refreshing the Consumption Item Mapping when a Field...
entry.content: Hi Eric,

entry.title: Re: Filtering on Association property in Fiori Element app via ABAP CDS Virtual Elements
entry.content:
Thank you for the informative blog @maheshpalavalli!

I found a way to also get this to work in a scenario with a one-to-many cardinality association, so I will share my solution here, hoping that anyone with the same requirement can benefit from it. 🙂

I have a composite RAP BO where I needed to add a filter to the list report of the parent entity, based on a field of the child entity.

In the implementation of the filter transformation method (if_sadl_exit_filter_transform~map_atom), I selected all entries from the child entity where the relevant field contains the filter value (iv_value). Next, I looped over the retrieved entries and, for each one, created a condition on the field used to match the association between the parent and the child. (In my scenario this is also the unique key of the parent entity, so I could directly filter on this field, but perhaps there are some use cases where it is necessary to first obtain the corresponding entries of the parent entity). I then chained these individual conditions together using the OR operator, as explained on this SAP Help page. Finally, I added a fallback condition to handle the case when no matching entries are found, ensuring that the filter is never empty.

Below is a generic code sample where you can replace the placeholders (e.g. <your_child_entity>, <matching_field>, <parent_key>, and <filter_field>) with your actual names.

 

METHOD if_sadl_exit_filter_transform~map_atom.

  DATA lt_child_entries TYPE TABLE OF <your_child_entity>.
  DATA ls_child_entry   TYPE <your_child_entity>.
  DATA lo_field_filter  TYPE REF TO if_sadl_simple_cond_element.
  DATA lo_condition     TYPE REF TO if_sadl_cond_provider_generic.

  " Get all entries from the child entity where <filter_field> equals iv_value
  SELECT <matching_field> FROM <your_child_entity>
    WHERE <filter_field> = _value
    INTO TABLE _child_entries.

  LOOP AT lt_child_entries INTO ls_child_entry.
    " Create a condition for the parent entity key field
    lo_field_filter = cl_sadl_cond_prov_factory_pub=>create_simple_cond_factory( )->element( '<parent_key>' ).
    lo_condition = lo_field_filter->equals( ls_child_entry-<matching_field> ).

    " Chain conditions with OR
    IF ro_condition IS INITIAL.
      ro_condition = lo_condition.
    ELSE.
      ro_condition = ro_condition->or( lo_condition ).
    ENDIF.

    CLEAR lo_field_filter.
  ENDLOOP.

  " Fallback: If no conditions were created, apply a default condition
  IF ro_condition IS INITIAL.
    lo_field_filter = cl_sadl_cond_prov_factory_pub=>create_simple_cond_factory( )->element( '<parent_key>' ).
    ro_condition = lo_field_filter->equals( '' ).
  ENDIF.

ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Re: Experience Your Favourite BI Platform, BOBJ, in a Fresh New Look with the SAP BI 2025 Release
entry.content:
It was forgotten to mention that a new license key is required for BI 2025. Also, this is not mentioned in the installation or upgrade guide, nor in any other guides or articles.
It seems like this was a last-minute decision to implement the new license routine.  or is it a software error in handling the existing BI 4.3 license?

entry.title: Re: Develop Powerful Analytical Queries using ABAP Core Data Services (CDS)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Mohn

You are right, the special “Data Preview” feature in the ABAP Development Tool (Eclipse) is not available in on prem systems 2023 or below. It is planned for the next on prem release (most likely 2025).

Instead, you can use the transaction RSRT in SAP GUI. It works similar to the preview.
The name of the query is “2C” followed by the CDS name of the analytical projection view. If the projection view has the header annotation Analytics.technicalName, then the name of the query is “2C” followed by this technical name. The annotation Analytics.technicalName should be used if an old query view (view entity with header annotation Analytics.query:true) is migrated to a projection view.

When you want to run an old query view, then you have to use the name "2C" followed by the SQL-view name.

To pass the authorisation check, your user needs authorization for SDDLVIEW ( DDLNAME = CDS name) or alternatively S_RS_COMP (RSZCOMPID = 2C-name).

Regards,

Stefan

entry.title: Re: Integrating with the Alerts System API in SAP Service Cloud V2
entry.content: Feature Flag is "feature.insights.externalAlerts"

entry.title: Re: Integration Center - A viable and flexible alternative for LMS Connectors !
entry.content: Hi there. Curious if by using the integration centre, can it determine whichever email is set as Primary in the employee's profile, and send that to the LMS?  For example, not all of our employees have both personal and business emails. Some will have one, some will have both.  But in the EC profile, one of the email types will be set as primary.  We need the primary email to be sent to the LMS.  Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Calculating Energy Consumption in the Product Carbon Footprint
entry.content:
Hello, 

thank you so much for your response!

I have tried processes 1-3 and a few questions have come up. Sadly, they didn't achieve what I was looking for and so I was wondering if maybe I expressed myself poorly or am simply misunderstood the purpose of the PCF.

The electricity used during production is scope 2, correct? I am trying to integrate those emissions into my product carbon footprint. Currently, I am only managing to calculate Scope 3.1 emissions in any PCF I have tried so far. Everything is just labeled "Purchased Goods and Services". The screenshot below shows the breakdown values for when I look at the production document that is imported via the template for the PCF. However, all other item footprints are the same (2 products, 1 supplier and the shown production document):

The Corporate Carbon Footprints seem to work. I can use Business Activities and Energy Bills for those easily (see picture below). I have set up an Energy Flow Model with Electricity as the source and then the Facility + an Assembly Resource each connected to the Source via a Meter. Those show up in the CCF, just not in the PCF in any way. I basically want to achieve the same for each product in the PCF instead of the CCF.

Am I perhaps misunderstand the use and purpose of the PCF? Is it not supposed to be used for this kind of thing?

Kind Regards

entry.title: Re: Data Services - Error on SQL Transact
entry.content:
@mtayibi It appears you redacted the actual error message returned by the database and the SQL query that was being executed.

Redact the PII and provide the resulting error message.

Regards,

Joe

entry.title: Unlock Your Potential with the Updated C4H320 - SAP Commerce Cloud Business User Training
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm glad to announce the release of our updated Instructor Lead Training course for SAP Commerce Cloud Business User Training.

C4H320 - SAP Commerce Cloud Business User Training

If you're looking to harness the full potential of SAP Commerce Cloud, the updated C4H320 - SAP Commerce Cloud Business User Training is an excellent opportunity for you. This instructor-led course is designed to empower business users with the skills and knowledge needed to effectively utilize SAP Commerce Cloud.

In this 3-day course, participants will learn about SAP Commerce features, concepts, and user interfaces from a functional perspective to get an all-round view of the software. This course contains demonstrations by trainers as well as hands-on exercises for course participants. The course presents a high-level overview of SAP Commerce Cloud and its important role in the SAP Customer Experience portfolio. Participants will have the opportunity to identify the main out-of-the-box features and perform exercises on how to provide basic configuration in Backoffice, SmartEdit and other administration tools.

Course Overview

The C4H320 training course is specifically tailored for business users who want to get the most out of SAP Commerce Cloud. Here’s a glimpse of what you’ll learn:

  • Understanding Core Features: Gain a deep insight into the core functionality of SAP Commerce Cloud. You will learn how to navigate the platform with ease and understand its primary components.
  • Enhancing User Experience: Discover how to enhance your customer's journey using SAP Commerce Cloud’s customizable storefront solutions and user-centric design features. Learn about strategies to improve user engagement and retention.
  • Omnichannel Integration: Learn how to create a seamless experience across multiple channels, ensuring your customers can engage with your business wherever they are, whether it’s mobile, online, or in-store.
  • Efficient Data Management: Understand how to manage data effectively within SAP Commerce Cloud for better business insights. Master data input, inventory management, and customer data handling to streamline operations.
  • Product and Content Administration: Equip yourself with knowledge about administering product catalogs and managing content efficiently, allowing you to update and adapt your offerings dynamically.

Benefits of Instructor-Led Training

Taking the C4H320 course as an instructor-led experience is a significant advantage:

  • Personalized Guidance: Direct interaction with experienced instructors provides personalized guidance and insights. Get answers to your questions in real-time and learn from practical examples tailored to your industry.
  • Hands-On Experience: Engaging in live demonstrations and interactive sessions ensures that you grasp concepts effectively and are well-equipped to implement them in real scenarios.
  • Networking Opportunities: Connect with other professionals with similar interests and share experiences. Collaborative learning creates a rich environment for exchanging ideas and solving common challenges.

Live System Access
The course is available for Live System Access, an easy way to perform the hands-on exercises of these courses via SAP systems. For more information, please refer to this blog post. If you want to enroll for this system you can go here.

entry.title: Re: SAC and Group Reporting Implementation Timing
entry.content:
Hello Sen,

Thank you for your quick response. I appreciate the information. I have a follow-up question regarding the statement that GR and SAC might experience a 'technical go-live' before the actual data flow.

To ensure I understand correctly, could you please clarify whether this 'technical go-live' refers to:

A deployment of GR and SAC before the SAP S/4HANA core goes live?
Parallel deployment of GR and SAC while the SAP S/4HANA core is already in production.
Furthermore, considering that we are not migrating historical data, I'm concerned about the potential impact on GR and SAC if they go live in parallel with S/4HANA. It seems that GR and SAC would require initial data, such as loaded balances or current transactions, from the S/4HANA core to function effectively. Could you please elaborate on how this scenario would be handled?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
entry.content:
@GregBotticchio It was forgotten to mention that a new license key is required for BI 2025. Also, this is not mentioned in the installation or upgrade guide, nor in any other guides or articles.
It seems like this was a last-minute decision to implement the new license routine.  or is it a software error in handling the existing BI 4.3 license?

entry.title: Re: Express Document "Update Was Terminated" Received fro...
entry.content:
Basically, when you receive an  "Update Was Terminated" message, execute SM13 to find the exact FM that triggered it during update. 

Often this is a print form error, and the FM or Dump will indicate Smart Forms  or Adobe: Generated Function Group Name (Form name in text of FG)

  • /1BCDWB/SF* for Smart Forms
  • /1BCDWB/SM* for Adobe Forms

entry.title: Active Ingredient Management in S/4HANA Cloud Public edition.
entry.content:
Hi,

Where can we find information on Active INgredienat Management of products? We manufacture products which have two units of measure  - litres and Litres of absolute alcohol. Each batch manufactured and sold requires both measures. The one is a percentage of the other (LAA is around 96.4% of L)

 

We will need to use these in production, batch management, stcok valuation as well as sales.

 

Many thanks

Rob

entry.title: FSCM CM: Merge Tax - SDCM/MM flow
entry.content:
Hi experts, 

Even tought I have flagged field MERGE_TAX in VFCLM_FB_CHN_STP customizing, items from MM and SD are been load in FQM_FLOW in 2 flow:

SDCM:  900000 Incoming Bank Cash and 900018 Incoming Bank Cash (Tax)

MM: 900001 Outgoing Bank Cash and 900019 Outgoing Bank Cash (Tax)

I've already applied SAP note: 3499992 - Flow Builder Plus: Merge tax items based on tax codes.

Has anyone faced this scenario? Could you please help me?

Thanks in advance.

 

BR

 

RL

entry.title: Re: Joule, SAP AI-copilot Available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in Early Adopter Care
entry.content: Hi is the Joule available in 3SL s4hana cloud public edition? and GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition basic ?

entry.title: Source of Supply Assignment differs for MRP and manually created Purchase Requisitions
entry.content:
Does anyone have more details on an issue we experienced in S4 Hana where the source of supply determination seems to work differently when manually creating PReqs vs PReqs created via MRP?
We are trying to implement simplified sourcing (eliminating the use of source lists) and have the MRP pick up a contract directly to be assigned to the created PReqs.
Expectation is (same as with using source lists) that source assignment is looking for a valid contract which is valid at the time of the delivery date specified in the PReq.
This is valid when manually creating the PReqs via ME51N.
However, when MRP creates the PReqs it is always the PReq creation date which determines the contract to be selected.

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
entry.content: The configuration link is no more active!

entry.title: Re: Pop-up alert option in SuccessFactors
entry.content: Hi there, we still do not have a solution. I forgot that I did create an enhancement request. Please consider voting! https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/258383/?section=sectionDetails
